UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user within the BidNiche marketplace, holding personal details and roles (buyer/seller).

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

address.description: The physical address of the user, used for shipping or billing.

role.description: The role of the user within the platform (e.g., 'buyer', 'seller', 'admin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Auction.title: Auction
Auction.description: Represents an auction listing created by a seller in the BidNiche marketplace.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Auction entity.

title.description: The title of the auction listing.

description.description: A detailed description of the item being auctioned.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images of the item.

startingPrice.description: The initial price from which bidding starts.

reservePrice.description: The minimum price the seller is willing to accept for the item. If not met, the item may not be sold.

currentHighestBid.description: The current highest bid amount for this auction.

endTimestamp.description: The date and time when the auction is scheduled to end.

sellerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the seller who created this auction. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Auction)

status.description: The current status of the auction (e.g., 'active', 'closed', 'cancelled').

winnerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the winning bidder, if the auction has closed with a winner. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:1 Auction)

winningBidId.description: Reference to the winning Bid for this auction, if applicable. (Relationship: Bid 1:1 Auction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the auction listing was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the auction listing was last updated.

Bid.title: Bid
Bid.description: Represents a bid placed by a buyer on an auction listing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Bid entity.

auctionId.description: Reference to the Auction this bid was placed on. (Relationship: Auction 1:N Bid)

bidderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the bidder. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Bid)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the bid.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the bid was placed.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The `userId` in the path ensures that only the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can access their own profile data, adhering to private data access modeling.

Private collection for each user's AI assistant, storing research or fact-finding data to build a personal knowledge base. Includes denormalized 'userId' in the document for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this AI corpus data.

params.name: corpusItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific item within the user's AI corpus.

definition.description: Stores public auction listings. Each document includes the 'sellerId' for authorization independence and 'status' for explicit state modeling, allowing for public reads and seller-specific writes. Supports listing of active auctions (QAPs).

params.name: auctionId
params.description: The unique identifier for an auction listing.

definition.description: Stores individual bids placed on a specific auction. Each bid document includes 'auctionId' and 'bidderId' for authorization independence and efficient querying of bid history for a given auction. Supports real-time updates and QAPs for bid history within an auction.

params.name: auctionId
params.description: The unique identifier of the auction to which this bid belongs.

params.name: bidId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific bid.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for BidNiche is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. 

1.  **UserProfile:** User profiles are stored in the `/users/{userId}` collection. This leverages path-based authorization, where `userId` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid`, making user-specific data access straightforward and secure without any `get()` calls. This adheres to the 'Private Data' access modeling principle.

2.  **AI Corpus:** To address the user's request for an AI to have its own dataset, a subcollection `/users/{userId}/aiCorpus/{corpusItemId}` is introduced. This path structure ensures that AI corpus data is strictly private and owned by the `userId` in the path. The `AICorpusItem` document itself will also include `userId` as a denormalized field, providing **Authorization Independence**. Rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` for granular write access, while the path itself restricts `list` operations to the owning user, supporting QAPs.

3.  **Auctions:** Public auction listings are stored in the `/auctions/{auctionId}` collection. Each `Auction` document includes `sellerId` and `status`. This is crucial for **Authorization Independence**: the `sellerId` field within the document allows security rules to instantly verify ownership (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.sellerId`) for update/delete operations without needing to fetch the seller's `UserProfile` document. The `status` field (`active`, `closed`) provides **Explicit State Modeling**, which enables robust **QAPs**. For example, rules can permit public read access to documents where `resource.data.status == 'active'`, efficiently supporting the 'Public Auction Display' feature. This also exemplifies **Structural Segregation** as it defines a homogenous security posture for *published* auctions, allowing public listing. Draft auctions, if introduced, would be segregated into a different, private collection.

4.  **Bids:** Bids are stored as a subcollection under their respective auctions: `/auctions/{auctionId}/bids/{bidId}`. Each `Bid` document contains `auctionId` and `bidderId`. This design ensures **Authorization Independence** as rules can directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.bidderId` for a bidder's own bids, and `resource.data.auctionId == auctionId` (from the path wildcard) to verify context, again without any `get()` calls to parent documents or other collections. This hierarchical structure naturally supports **QAPs** for 'Bid History' by allowing efficient listing of all bids for a specific auction (`/auctions/{auctionId}/bids`), with rules dictating read permissions (e.g., all authenticated users can read, only the bidder can create).
